title: Variables
description: Bind names to values and update them safely.

concept.definition: Python variables are references to objects.
concept.analogy: A variable is a sticky label pointing to a value box.

title: Core Data Types
description: Use int, float, str, bool and basic containers.

concept.definition: Python provides built-in types for numbers, text, logic, and collections.
concept.analogy: Types are value families with shared behavior.

defaultCode:
age = 20
score = 9.5
name = 'Ari'
active = True
print(age, score, name, active)

title: Functions
description: Define reusable logic with parameters and return values.

concept.definition: Functions package repeatable logic into callable blocks.
concept.analogy: A function is a recipe you can run many times.

title: Control Flow

concept.definition: Conditional statements choose which code path runs.
concept.analogy: Conditions are decision gates in a flowchart.

title: Loops
description: Repeat logic with for and while loops.

concept.definition: Loops run code repeatedly over ranges or while conditions hold.
concept.analogy: A loop is a conveyor repeating a task.

defaultCode:
total = 0
for n in [1, 2, 3]:
    total += n
print(total)

title: Lists & Tuples
description: Store ordered data with mutable and immutable sequences.

concept.definition: Lists are mutable ordered sequences; tuples are immutable ordered sequences.
concept.analogy: Lists are editable checklists, tuples are locked records.

title: Dictionaries & Sets
description: Use key-value mappings and unique-value collections.

concept.definition: Dictionaries map keys to values; sets store unique unordered values.
concept.analogy: dict is a lookup table, set is a uniqueness filter.
keyPoints: both are common in data tasks

title: Classes & Objects
description: Model state and behavior with classes.

concept.definition: Classes define object structure and methods; instances are runtime objects.
concept.analogy: Class is blueprint, object is built unit.

title: Exceptions

concept.definition: Exceptions represent runtime failures and can be caught for graceful recovery.
concept.analogy: try/except is a safety net around risky code.

title: Modules & Packages
description: Organize code and import reusable functionality.

concept.definition: Modules are single Python files; packages group modules into directories.
concept.analogy: Modules are chapters, packages are bookshelves.
keyPoints: from x import y imports names

title: File I/O
description: Read and write files safely using context managers.

concept.definition: Use with-open blocks to handle file resources automatically.
concept.analogy: with is auto-close mode for file handles.

defaultCode:
with open('note.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('hello')
with open('note.txt', 'r') as f:
    print(f.read())

title: Comprehensions
description: Build lists, sets, and dicts concisely with expressions.

concept.definition: Comprehensions create collections from iterable expressions in one compact form.
concept.analogy: Comprehensions are mini data pipelines in a single line.

defaultCode:
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4]
squares = [n * n for n in nums if n % 2 == 0]
print(squares)
